mentions.note: Long-term bull on Robinhood's younger, wealth-inheriting user base, but expects near-term trading to keep tracking crypto and speculative names lower.
Captured young retail cohort mindset; sticky product resonates with millennial investors.
Too tightly correlated to Bitcoin; crypto no longer store of value, not a currency, just speculative—toxic repository.
Trading at 30x earnings with excessive crypto exposure (~50% of business); will remain volatile and trade like Bitcoin until balance shifts toward equities/bonds.
Sixth-worst S&P performer in February, down 24%, on miserable quarter (soft options/crypto trading); now hostage to crypto and predictions betting; Cramer prefers SoFi for young people fintech.
Rallied alongside Bitcoin and high-risk tech; institutional buying of riskiest assets confirms sentiment inflection from fear to risk appetite.
At $79.80 (caller Cheryl bought at $145, $120): Buy at current levels; would buy even lower at $70; focus on future, not cost basis.

Newly listed venture cap fund; launched March 6, dropped 16% on day 1, now back above IPO. NAV $25.31 vs. stock price ~$26 — reasonable but not compelling. Only 7 holdings, mostly weak except Databricks; Cramer prefers Fundrise.
Trades at 28x earnings vs. Morgan Stanley at 14x; fast-growing but lacks sticky money; room to go lower until war ends.
Terrific quarter expected given volatility; still waiting for company to attract serious institutional investors beyond traders.
